# Firestore Setup for Voting App

## Database Structure

Your app needs two collections in Firestore:

### 1. `polls` collection
This stores all poll options.

**Document ID**: Auto-generated by Firestore
**Document structure**:
```
{
  text: string,           // The poll option text
  createdBy: string,      // User ID who created the option
  createdAt: timestamp    // When the option was created
}
```

**Example**:
```
polls/
  ├── poll_id_1/
  │   ├── text: "Option A"
  │   ├── createdBy: "user123"
  │   └── createdAt: 2024-05-01...
  └── poll_id_2/
      ├── text: "Option B"
      ├── createdBy: "user456"
      └── createdAt: 2024-05-01...
```

### 2. `votes` collection
This stores all votes, ensuring one vote per user per option.

**Document ID**: Auto-generated by Firestore
**Document structure**:
```
{
  userId: string,      // User ID who voted
  pollId: string,      // ID of the poll option they voted for
  votedAt: timestamp   // When they voted
}
```

**Example**:
```
votes/
  ├── vote_id_1/
  │   ├── userId: "user123"
  │   ├── pollId: "poll_id_1"
  │   └── votedAt: 2024-05-01...
  └── vote_id_2/
      ├── userId: "user123"
      ├── pollId: "poll_id_2"
      └── votedAt: 2024-05-01...
```

## Firestore Security Rules

Replace your Firestore security rules with these rules to ensure:
- Users can only read all polls
- Users can only add new poll options when authenticated
- Users can only vote when authenticated
- Votes cannot be deleted (prevents tampering)

Go to **Firebase Console** → **Firestore Database** → **Rules** and paste:

```javascript
rules_version = '2';
service cloud.firestore {
  match /databases/{database}/documents {
    // Polls collection - Anyone can read, only authenticated users can create
    match /polls/{pollId} {
      allow read: if true;
      allow create: if request.auth != null && 
                       request.resource.data.text is string &&
                       request.resource.data.text.size() > 0;
      allow update, delete: if false;
    }

    // Votes collection - One vote per user per option
    match /votes/{voteId} {
      allow read: if request.auth != null;
      allow create: if request.auth != null && 
                       request.resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid &&
                       request.resource.data.pollId is string &&
                       request.resource.data.votedAt is timestamp &&
                       !exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/votes/$(request.resource.data.userId)_$(request.resource.data.pollId));
      allow update, delete: if false;
    }
  }
}
```

## How the Voting App Works

1. **Sign In**: Users sign in with email/password or Google
2. **View Polls**: All polls are displayed with real-time vote counts
3. **Add Poll**: Authenticated users can add new poll options
4. **Vote**: Users can vote for each option. After voting, their vote is saved to Firestore and:
   - The button shows "✓ You voted" and is disabled
   - The vote count updates in real-time
   - They cannot vote again for that option
5. **Progress Bar**: Shows the percentage of votes for each option, updated in real-time

## Creating Collections Manually (if needed)

If the collections don't exist, Firestore will create them automatically when you first add data. However, you can create them manually:

1. Go to **Firebase Console**
2. Click on **Firestore Database**
3. Click **+ Start Collection**
4. Create collection named `polls`
5. Add a test document (or skip)
6. Repeat for `votes` collection

## Testing

To test the voting system:

1. Sign in with one account
2. Add a poll option
3. Vote for it
4. Try voting again (should be prevented)
5. Sign out and sign in with a different account
6. Vote for the same option (should work)
7. See real-time vote updates and progress bars
